prompt: For sample S003, the PL (phred-scaled genotype likelihood) field shows three values corresponding to genotypes 0/0, 0/1, and 1/1. Report (a) the three PL values, (b) which genotype is most likely (PL=0 marks the most likely), and (c) whether the most likely genotype matches the GT field.
context:
#CHROM	POS	ID	REF	ALT	QUAL	FILTER	INFO	FORMAT	S001	S002	S003
chr03	1538742	.	C	T	289	PASS	AF=0.33;AN=6;AC=2	GT:GQ:DP:AD:PL	0/0:40:25:25,0:0,75,840	1/1:38:22:0,22:690,66,0	0/1:28:19:8,11:180,0,95

Note: PL values are phred-scaled likelihoods for genotypes 0/0, 0/1, 1/1. Lower PL = more likely genotype. PL=0 marks the most likely genotype.
correct_answer: (a) PL=180,0,95; (b) 0/1 is most likely (PL=0); (c) Yes, GT=0/1 matches.
rubric: Must report PL=180,0,95 for S003, identify 0/1 as most likely (PL=0), and confirm GT=0/1 matches.
notes: Tests understanding of PL (phred-scaled likelihood) field ordering and interpretation. PL=0 marks the most likely genotype; values are ordered 0/0, 0/1, 1/1 for biallelic sites.
